name: Chewy Praline Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Spray a 15x10x1-inch jelly roll pan with cooking spray.
3. In a small bowl, combine flour and salt.
4. In a large bowl, beat together butter, shortening, and brown sugar until smooth and creamy.
5. Beat in egg and vanilla extract. Stir in the flour mixture and pecans.
6. Spread the mixture evenly in the prepared pan.
7. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until lightly browned and a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean. Remove from oven and let cool on a rack.
8. While the bars are cooling, melt baking chips at the top of a double boiler over hot, not boiling water, stirring until smooth.
9. Drizzle the melted chips over the cooled bars.
10. Cut into bars and store in a tightly sealed plastic container with sheets of waxed paper between layers. If not serving immediately, do not cut or drizzle with chips. Cover tightly with heavy-duty foil; seal in a heavy-duty plastic bag or place the pan inside a large plastic container and seal. Freeze if not serving immediately. To serve, bring to room temperature and drizzle with melted white baking chips.
